info.title: Tinday API
info.description:
Tinday is a professional networking application that uses a swipe-based interface to connect professionals. This API provides user authentication, profile management, swipe-based matching, real-time messaging, AI-powered recommendations, and an AI chat assistant with RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation).

## Authentication
All authenticated endpoints require a Supabase JWT passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. Obtain a token via the `/api/auth/login` or `/api/auth/register` endpoints. Tokens expire and can be refreshed via `/api/auth/refresh`.

## Rate Limiting
- **Global**: 100 requests per minute per IP
- **Auth endpoints** (`/api/auth/*`): 10 requests per minute per IP

## Real-time Features
- **Messages**: Subscribe to Supabase Realtime on the `messages` table for new message delivery
- **Presence & Typing**: Connect via Socket.io with JWT auth for typing indicators and online presence

## AI Features
- Vector-based profile embeddings (768-dimensional Gemini embeddings)
- Dynamic recommendations using cosine similarity and preference learning
- AI chat assistant with RAG context from relevant profiles

## Common Error Responses
- `400` - Bad Request (invalid input, business rule violation)
- `401` - Unauthorized (missing/invalid/expired JWT)
- `403` - Forbidden (not a member of the requested match)
- `404` - Not Found (resource does not exist)
- `409` - Conflict (duplicate swipe, duplicate email)
- `422` - Validation Error (Zod schema validation failure with field-level details)
- `429` - Too Many Requests (rate limit exceeded)
- `500` - Internal Server Error
- `502` - Bad Gateway (FastAPI AI service unavailable)

tags.name: Auth
tags.description: User registration, login, token refresh, logout, and current user retrieval. Rate-limited at 10 req/min.

tags.name: Profiles
tags.description: User profile management: view, update, search profiles, and upload avatars/project media.

tags.name: Swipes
tags.description: Swipe actions (RIGHT/LIKE or LEFT/PASS) on other profiles. Mutual RIGHT swipes create a match.

tags.name: Matches
tags.description: List matches where mutual RIGHT swipes occurred between two users.

tags.name: Messaging
tags.description: Inbox listing, sending messages within a match, and retrieving message history with pagination.

tags.name: Explore
tags.description: AI-powered profile discovery feed that excludes already-swiped users and ranks by preference similarity.

tags.name: AI
tags.description: AI-powered features: recommendations, conversational chat with RAG, and profile analysis.

bearerAuth.description: Supabase-issued JWT access token. Obtained from /api/auth/login or /api/auth/register. Include as: Authorization: Bearer <token>

User.description: Supabase Auth user object returned on registration/login

Session.description: Supabase session containing JWT and refresh token

access_token.description: JWT access token for Bearer authentication

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp when the token expires

refresh_token.description: Long-lived refresh token for obtaining new access tokens

AuthResponse.description: Response for login and token refresh

Profile.description: Complete user profile. The embedding vector is internal and never returned to clients.

id.description: Profile ID (same as auth user ID)

avatar_url.description: Public URL of the user's avatar image in Supabase Storage

roles.description: List of professional roles/titles (e.g., ['Software Engineer', 'Tech Lead'])

projects.description: JSON array of project objects. Schema is flexible - each project may contain title, description, url, media, etc.

preferences.description: JSON object for user preferences. Schema is flexible.

PublicProfile.description: Public-facing profile (excludes internal fields like preferences and embeddings)

refresh_token.description: Refresh token obtained from login or previous refresh

UpdateProfileRequest.description: All fields are optional. Only provided fields will be updated. Updating triggers a re-embedding of the profile via the AI service.

preferences.description: User preference settings as a free-form JSON object

receiver_id.description: UUID of the profile being swiped on

match.description: The created match if this swipe resulted in a mutual RIGHT swipe. null if no match was created.

MatchWithUser.description: A match including the other user's profile

read_at.description: Timestamp when the message was read by the recipient. null if unread.

latest_message.description: Most recent message in this conversation, or null if no messages yet

unread_count.description: Number of unread messages from the other user

last_activity.description: Timestamp of the latest message or match creation. Used for sorting.

muted.description: Whether the current user has muted notifications for this conversation

MatchUserState.description: The current user's per-conversation inbox state.

muted.description: Whether notifications for this conversation are muted

deleted_at.description: When the conversation was soft-hidden, or null if visible. The chat reappears once newer activity arrives.

message.description: The user's chat message to the AI assistant

conversation_history.description: Previous conversation turns for context. Each turn has a role and content.

role.description: Who sent the message: user (you), model (AI), or system (instructions)

profile_id.description: UUID of the profile to analyze

analysis.description: AI-generated analysis of why this profile might be a good match

total.description: Total number of profiles matching the search criteria

BadRequest.description: Bad Request - Invalid input or business rule violation

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Missing, invalid, or expired JWT token

Forbidden.description: Forbidden - Authenticated user does not have access to this resource

example.error: Access denied: Not a member of this match

NotFound.description: Not Found - The requested resource does not exist

example.message: The requested resource was not found.

Conflict.description: Conflict - Duplicate resource or constraint violation

example.message: A record with this information already exists.

details.message: String must contain at least 8 character(s)

TooManyRequests.description: Too Many Requests - Rate limit exceeded

example.error: Too many requests, please try again later.

BadGateway.description: Bad Gateway - AI service is unavailable

get.description: Returns the current status of the Express server. Does not require authentication. Useful for load balancers and monitoring.

post.description:
Creates a new user account and profile. The user is created in Supabase Auth with email confirmation automatically set (email_confirm: true). A corresponding row is inserted into the profiles table. A fire-and-forget call is made to the FastAPI embed service to generate the initial vector embedding from the user's name.

**Rate limited**: 10 requests per minute per IP.

201.description: Account created successfully. Returns the Supabase user object.

post.summary: Log in with email and password
post.description:
Authenticates a user with email and password. Returns a Supabase session containing an access_token (JWT), refresh_token, and user object. The access_token should be passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header for all authenticated requests. Tokens expire after the duration specified in `expires_in`.

**Authentication flow**: The returned `access_token` is a Supabase JWT. Pass it as `Authorization: Bearer <access_token>`. When expired, use the `refresh_token` with `/api/auth/refresh` to obtain a new session.

**Rate limited**: 10 requests per minute per IP.

200.description: Login successful. Returns session with tokens.

post.description:
Exchanges a refresh token for a new session with a fresh access_token and refresh_token. Use this when the access_token has expired instead of requiring the user to log in again.

**Rate limited**: 10 requests per minute per IP.

example.message: Session expired. Please log in again.

post.summary: Log out and invalidate the current session
post.description: Signs out the current user by invalidating their JWT on the Supabase server side. Requires a valid Bearer token. After logout, the token cannot be used again.

get.summary: Get the currently authenticated user's profile
get.description:
Returns the full profile (from the profiles table) of the currently authenticated user. This is equivalent to `GET /api/profiles/me` but lives under the auth namespace. Requires a valid Bearer token.

**Not rate limited** beyond the global 100 req/min limit.
Returns the full profile of the currently authenticated user, including internal fields like preferences.

put.description: Partially updates the authenticated user's profile. All fields are optional - only provided fields will be updated. After a successful update, a fire-and-forget call is made to the FastAPI embed service to regenerate the profile's vector embedding from the concatenated about, skills, roles, and preferences text. This keeps the AI recommendations up to date.

value.about: Senior full-stack developer with 8 years of experience

example.name: Jane Doe
example.about: Senior full-stack developer with 8 years of experience

post.description:
Uploads a profile avatar image. The request body must be raw binary image data with the appropriate Content-Type header (e.g., `image/jpeg`, `image/png`). The image is stored in the `avatars` Supabase Storage bucket and the profile's `avatar_url` is updated with the public URL. Maximum file size: 5 MB.

**Content-Type**: Must be an image MIME type (`image/*`).

schema.description: Raw image binary data (JPEG, PNG, WebP, etc.). Max 5 MB.

post.description:
Uploads an image or video file for a project. The request body must be raw binary data with the appropriate Content-Type header. The file is stored in the `projects` Supabase Storage bucket and a public URL is returned. Maximum file size: 10 MB.

**Content-Type**: Must be an image or video MIME type (`image/*` or `video/*`).

schema.description: Raw image binary data. Max 10 MB.
Raw video binary data. Max 10 MB.

200.description: Media uploaded successfully. Returns the public URL.

get.description:
Searches profiles by skills (contains match) and experience range. Supports pagination. This is a direct database search (not AI-powered). For AI-powered recommendations, use `/api/explore` or `/api/ai/recommend`.

**Query parameters**:
- `skills`: A skill string or array of skill strings. Profiles are matched if they contain ALL specified skills (Supabase `contains` operator).
- `min_experience`: Minimum years of experience (inclusive).
- `max_experience`: Maximum years of experience (inclusive).
- `page`: Page number (1-based, default 1).
- `limit`: Results per page (default 10).

parameters.name: skills
parameters.description: Filter profiles that have all specified skills. Can be passed multiple times or as comma-separated values.

get.summary: Get a specific user's public profile
get.description: Returns the public profile of a user by their UUID. Private fields (preferences, embeddings) are excluded from the response.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: UUID of the user profile to fetch

post.summary: Swipe on a profile (like or pass)
post.description:
Records a swipe action (RIGHT = like, LEFT = pass) on another user's profile. Business rules:
- You cannot swipe on yourself (returns 400).
- You cannot swipe the same user twice (returns 409).
- A RIGHT swipe checks for a mutual RIGHT swipe from the other user. If found, a match is created and returned in the response.
- After recording, a fire-and-forget call updates the user's AI preference vector via the FastAPI service.

**Match creation**: Matches are created with ordered user IDs (`user_a_id < user_b_id`) to ensure consistency. The match object is returned alongside the swipe when a mutual match occurs.

201.description: Swipe recorded. If a mutual match was created, the `match` field will contain the match object.

get.summary: List all matches for the current user
get.description: Returns all matches where the authenticated user is either `user_a` or `user_b`. Results are sorted by creation date descending (newest first). Each match includes the other user's public profile. Matches with deleted/null profiles are filtered out.

200.description: List of matches with the other user's profile

get.description:
Returns a ranked list of profiles for the current user to swipe on. The feed is generated by the AI recommendation engine which considers:
1. The user's learned preference vector (updated with each swipe)
2. Vector similarity between the user's preference and candidate profiles
3. Optional skill and experience filters

Already-swiped profiles are automatically excluded. The response preserves the AI-ranked order.

**Query parameters**:
- `skills`: Filter by skills (optional).
- `min_experience`: Minimum years of experience (optional).
- `max_experience`: Maximum years of experience (optional).
- `limit`: Max results to return (default 20).

**Difference from `/api/ai/recommend`**: Both use the same AI backend, but `/api/explore` is the primary swipe feed endpoint while `/api/ai/recommend` is a lower-level proxy.

parameters.name: skills
parameters.description: Filter profiles by skills. Can be passed multiple times.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of profiles to return

example.name: John Smith
example.about: Backend developer specializing in distributed systems

projects.title: Microservices Platform
projects.description: Built a microservices platform handling 10M req/day

get.description:
Returns all match conversations for the current user, sorted by most recent activity. Each entry includes:
- The other user's profile
- The latest message (or null if no messages exist yet)
- Count of unread messages from the other user
- Timestamp of last activity

Results are sorted by `last_activity` descending (most recent first).

200.description: Inbox with conversations sorted by most recent activity

latest_message.content: Hey! Would love to connect about your AI projects.

get.description:
Returns paginated messages for a specific match, sorted by creation time ascending (oldest first). The authenticated user must be a member of the match (403 if not, 404 if match doesn't exist).

**Auto-read**: Messages sent by the other user that are unread will be marked as read (fire-and-forget update).

**Pagination**: Use `from` (inclusive) and `to` (inclusive) for cursor-based pagination. Default range is 0-49.

example.content: Hi John, I saw we matched!
Hey! Would love to connect about your AI projects.

post.summary: Send a message in a match
post.description:
Sends a text message in a specific match conversation. The authenticated user must be a member of the match. The message is broadcast to the match room via Supabase Realtime so the recipient receives it instantly if subscribed.

**Real-time delivery**: The recipient should subscribe to the `messages` table via Supabase Realtime for the specific `match_id` to receive messages in real time. The HTTP response returns the created message object.

parameters.name: matchId
parameters.description: UUID of the match to send a message to

value.content: Hey! Would love to connect about your AI projects.

example.content: Hey! Would love to connect about your AI projects.

put.description: Sets the authenticated user's mute preference for a match. Muting suppresses notifications (no notification row and no web push) for new messages in this conversation across all of the user's devices; messages still arrive and still count toward `unread_count`. Idempotent — the request carries the desired state. The user must be a member of the match (403 if not, 404 if it doesn't exist).

Sets the authenticated user's soft-delete state for a match. Hiding (`hidden: true`) removes the conversation from the user's inbox without deleting the match or any messages; it reappears automatically once the other user sends a new message. Restoring (`hidden: false`) un-hides it immediately, supporting an Undo. Per-user and synced across devices. The user must be a member of the match (403 if not, 404 if it doesn't exist).

hidden.description: true to hide (delete) the chat, false to restore it

get.description:
Returns AI-ranked profile recommendations using vector similarity. This is a lower-level proxy to the FastAI recommendation engine. Already-swiped users are automatically excluded. Results include full profiles sorted by relevance.

**Query parameters**:
- `skills`: Optional skill filter.
- `min_experience`: Minimum years of experience.
- `max_experience`: Maximum years of experience.
- `limit`: Max results (default 20).

post.description:
Initiates a conversation with the AI assistant. The AI uses RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) to find semantically similar profiles and provide networking advice based on real user profiles in the system.

**Response format**: Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream with Content-Type: text/event-stream. Each event contains a chunk of the AI's response. Accumulate all chunks to form the complete response.

**Conversation history**: Pass previous turns in conversation_history for multi-turn conversations. Each turn has a role (user, model, or system) and content.

**SSE format**: data: <text_chunk> followed by double newline

value.message: Find me someone with experience in machine learning who might be a good mentor.
Tell me more about their background.

conversation_history.content: Find me someone with experience in machine learning who might be a good mentor.
I found several profiles with ML experience. Dr. Sarah Chen has 10 years of experience in NLP and has mentored 5 junior engineers...

200.description: SSE stream of AI response chunks

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream. Each event is: data: <chunk>\n\n

text/event-stream.example:
data: Based on your

data:  interests in machine learning,

data:  I found a few profiles

data:  that could be great matches...

post.summary: Get AI analysis of a profile
post.description:
Returns an AI-generated analysis of why a specific profile might be a good match for the current user. The AI examines the profile's skills, experience, roles, and projects, then provides a personalized assessment.

**How it works**: The service fetches the profile, constructs a prompt asking the AI to analyze it, streams the response through the FastAPI chat service, accumulates the full response, and returns it as a JSON object with the `analysis` field.

example.analysis: John Smith has 7 years of experience in backend development with skills in Python, Go, and Kubernetes. Given your interest in distributed systems, John's experience building microservices platforms handling 10M requests/day would be an excellent learning opportunity. His background in AWS complements your cloud computing interests.
